issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the default focus ring without providing a visible alternative (box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline). Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus. The outline-offset property alone does not create a visible indicator when outline-width is zero.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and visible overflow, failing to visually hide content while keeping it in the accessibility tree. This renders the content visible to all users instead of hiding it visually for screen-reader-only announcement.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card

issuesFound.fix: Line 739: #search-input::placeholder { color: var(--colour-neutral-600); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on input hint text
issuesFound.explanation: The .input-hint class uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on a white background, providing only ~2.9:1 contrast. This fails WCAG 2.2 AA requirements and makes helper text difficult to perceive.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1609: .input-hint { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-600); ... }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset, which renders no visible focus ring. This blocks keyboard users from identifying focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the focus ring for keyboard users on buttons.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"]

issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, failing to hide content visually while keeping it accessible. It leaves content visible.

issuesFound.explanation: The step connector uses transform: translateY, but no reduced-motion override exists to disable this spatial animation.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the focus indicator in Windows High Contrast Mode where box-shadow is suppressed. Users relying on forced colors lose all focus visibility.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes the outline in focus-visible state with only outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for keyboard users activating the menu.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24 px minimum touch target size and far below the 44 px recommended size, increasing risk of mis-taps for users with tremors or limited dexterity.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have min-height: 24px, meeting the absolute minimum but falling below the 44 px recommended touch target size without compensating padding, potentially causing difficulty for users with limited motor control.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links ([id$="-submenu"] li a) have outline: none with negative outline-offset in focus-visible state, providing no visible focus ring when navigating expanded navigation menus via keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which fails to visually hide content while keeping it accessible to assistive technologies. Content remains visible on screen, breaking the layout, or if fixed, display: none or visibility: hidden would remove it from the accessibility tree entirely.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with zero padding, resulting in a 28×28 px touch target below the 44×44 px recommended size and below the 24×24 px minimum when padding is considered, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate accurately.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon-only buttons have min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, falling below the 24 px minimum touch target requirement and creating accessibility barriers for users who rely on large touch targets.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs have width: 20px, height: 20px, and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24 px minimum touch target size, making selection difficult for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text horizontally and reduces readability for users with dyslexia or low vision by crowding words together.
issuesFound.fix: Line 318:   word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer address links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer address links use color #64748b (var(--colour-neutral-500)) against background #0f172a (var(--colour-neutral-900)), yielding approximately 4.0:1 contrast, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text and making links difficult to perceive for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove outline in focus-visible state with only outline-offset, failing to provide a visible focus indicator for keyboard users accessing login or account sections.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search form submit button removes outline in focus-visible state with only outline-offset, leaving keyboard users without indication when the button has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class removes outline in focus-visible state with only outline-offset, leaving all button instances without a visible keyboard focus indicator unless overridden by specific button variants.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class includes transform in its transition property to animate the :active state (translateY(1px)), but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override exists to disable this spatial movement for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA primary button animates transform (translateY(-1px)) on hover via transition, but lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override to suppress this spatial movement for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses transform: translateY(-200%) and transition: transform to animate into view, but lacks a prefers-reduced-motion media query to disable this animation for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The primary button uses foreground #ffffff on background #5588bb (var(--colour-brand-primary)), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.5:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text (4.5:1) and UI components (3:1 against adjacent colors).

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on global anchor elements
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links by default. Many link instances (header navigation, account nav, footer links) rely solely on color change for differentiation, violating SC 1.4.1 Use of Color as color alone cannot distinguish links from surrounding text for users with color vision deficiencies.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as a box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline. This removes the keyboard focus ring for all interactive elements that do not have a more specific override, making keyboard navigation impossible to track.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px, but outline-offset alone is not visible when the outline width is zero. No box-shadow or border is provided in this rule to indicate focus state for buttons.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement. The base .nav-toggle border does not change on focus, so keyboard users cannot distinguish the focused state from the default state.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown link focus state sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Users navigating the submenu with a keyboard will not see which item is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search button focus state sets outline: none without a visible alternative. The base button has no border, so the focus state is invisible.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation links set outline: none on focus without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify the focused link in the account menu.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px. With box-sizing: border-box, the total clickable area is 20px, which is below the 24px minimum (WCAG 2.2 SC 2.5.8) and significantly below the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-width/min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px. Due to box-sizing: border-box, the padding is contained within this 20px box, resulting in a touch target smaller than the 24px minimum.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"] width/height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is explicitly sized to 28px × 28px with 0 padding. This is below the 44px recommended touch target size, making it difficult for touch users to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] width/height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input is explicitly sized to 20px × 20px. This is below the 24px minimum touch target size, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to toggle.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a min-height 24px
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px with no compensating padding. This is below the 44px recommended touch target size, increasing the risk of mis-taps on mobile devices.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and width/height: auto, which fails to remove the content from the visual layout. Content intended for screen readers only will be visible to sighted users, cluttering the interface.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for .hero-image img is empty. It does not disable the transform rotation or transition defined in the base rule, causing motion for users who requested reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing reduces the space between words, which can make text difficult to read for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #cbd5e1 / background #f8fafc
issuesFound.explanation: The "Not included" and "Not available" table cells use text colour #cbd5e1 on a background of #f8fafc. The contrast ratio is approximately 1.7:1, which fails the 3:1 minimum for large text (WCAG 1.4.3).

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have text-decoration: none and rely solely on colour (#94a3b8) to distinguish them from surrounding text (#334155). Users with colour vision deficiencies may not perceive them as links.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector inherits outline: none from the base :focus-visible rule and only sets outline-offset: -3px without providing a replacement border or box-shadow, resulting in no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability

issuesFound.fix: Line 242: `body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets outline-color but inherits outline: none from the base rule, resulting in no visible outline because outline-width is zero. No box-shadow or border replacement is provided.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which makes the content visible in layout and fails to hide it visually while keeping it accessible to assistive technologies. It should use position: absolute, 1px dimensions, and clip: rect(0,0,0,0).
issuesFound.fix: Line 390-400: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has width: 28px and height: 28px with padding: 0, resulting in a 28px touch target that is below the 44px recommended size and lacks sufficient padding to compensate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 548: `#site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon buttons

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to select.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing an alternative non-colour visual differentiator (such as border-bottom or font-weight change) for many link instances, causing links to be distinguished by colour alone.
issuesFound.fix: Line 252: `a { text-decoration: underline; }` or add alternative differentiator such as `border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor;` for link hover/focus states in component styles.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with no replacement style, removing the focus indicator. The box-shadow defined on :focus (line 538) does not apply to :focus-visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 543: `#search-input:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible sets outline: none and outline-offset: -3px without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border, leaving keyboard users without a focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 503: `[id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px without a visible replacement (box-shadow or border), resulting in no focus indicator for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 554: `#site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, providing no visible focus indicator replacement for keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, leaving buttons without a visible focus indicator for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 581: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (line 584) and uses transform: translateY(1px) on :active (line 589), but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this motion for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 584: Add inside existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block or create new: `.btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); }` and `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn:active { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible (forced-colors)
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, :focus-visible sets outline: none without a visible alternative. In high-contrast modes, this may render the focus indicator invisible if the UA does not override it.

issuesFound.explanation: The search input removes outline on focus-visible without a visible alternative. While :focus has a box-shadow, :focus-visible specifically targets keyboard users and must have a visible ring.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block overrides :focus-visible to remove the outline (outline: none) without providing a visible alternative. Box-shadow is suppressed in forced-colors mode, so users relying on Windows High Contrast Mode will have no focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px. Outline-offset alone does not create a visible focus ring, leaving keyboard users with no visible indication of focus. The border-radius property does not compensate for the missing outline.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. While padding increases the total height to approximately 32px, the explicit min-height declaration creates a risk of insufficient touch target if padding is removed or overridden.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set min-height: 24px, which meets the minimum but is below the 44px recommended touch target size. No padding is provided to increase the interactive area.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button focus-visible rule removes the default outline (outline: none) and only sets outline-offset: 3px without providing a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border. This leaves buttons without any visible focus indicator for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which makes the content visible rather than hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. Additionally, overflow: visible prevents clipping. This breaks the intended screen-reader-only behavior.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto — this does NOT hide content visually at all. Content marked as screen-reader-only will be visible to sighted users, defeating the purpose of the utility class.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link has transform: translateY(-200%) and transition: transform for its reveal animation. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 493 is EMPTY and does not disable this animation. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the skip link animate.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element has word-spacing: -0.2em which reduces space between words. Negative word-spacing can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia, low vision, or cognitive impairments who rely on clear word boundaries.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on body

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors mode without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block sets outline: none on :focus-visible without providing a visible alternative. In Windows High Contrast Mode, box-shadow is suppressed, making outline the only reliable focus indicator. Removing it leaves keyboard users with no visible focus indication.
issuesFound.fix: Line 307: Replace `outline: none;` with `outline: 3px solid ButtonText;` or `outline: 3px solid currentColor;` to ensure visibility in high-contrast mode.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow, border, or a non-zero outline value. This removes the focus indicator for keyboard users across all interactive elements that inherit this style, making it impossible to see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 295: Replace `outline: none;` with `outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);` or add `box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus);` to provide a visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body text
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words below the default. This negatively impacts readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities by making it harder to distinguish between words.
issuesFound.fix: Line 108: Remove `word-spacing: -0.2em;` or change to `word-spacing: normal;` to restore default readable spacing.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on header logo link
issuesFound.explanation: The .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the focus indicator without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users cannot see when the logo link has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 562: Add `outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);` or `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring);` to restore visibility.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector includes transition: transform var(--transition-slower), which causes spatial movement animation. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 423 is empty and does not override this transition, potentially causing vestibular issues for users sensitive to motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 423: Add `.hero-image img { transition: none; }` or `.hero-image img { transform: none !important; }` inside the prefers-reduced-motion media query to disable the animation.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on small button variant
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm selector sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. This makes the button difficult or impossible for users with motor impairments to activate reliably.
issuesFound.fix: Line 629: Change `min-height: 20px;` to `min-height: 24px;` or greater, or ensure the element has sufficient padding to achieve a 24px minimum dimension.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on featured pricing card
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover selector includes transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01), causing spatial movement on hover. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 1180 only covers .pricing-card:hover, not the featured variant, leaving this animation active for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1180: Add `.pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; }` inside the prefers-reduced-motion media query.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on feature card links
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a selector sets color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) on background-color: var(--colour-bg-page) (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.8:1. This is below the 4.5:1 minimum required for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 990: Change the color to `color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover);` (#004aaa) or add `text-decoration: underline;` to provide a non-colour visual differentiator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on mobile navigation toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the focus indicator without providing a visible alternative. This button is critical for mobile navigation and must have a visible focus state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 590: Add `outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);` or `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring);` to provide focus visibility.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the focus indicator without providing a visible alternative. This icon button has no text content and relies entirely on focus visibility for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 684: Add `outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);` or `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring);` to provide a visible focus ring.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the focus indicator without providing a visible alternative. Keyboard users navigating dropdown menus cannot see which link has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 640: Add `outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);` or `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring);` to restore focus visibility.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] selector sets width: 28px and height: 28px (within 24-43px range) but has zero padding, resulting in a touch target that may be smaller than the recommended 44px and lacks sufficient spacing to prevent accidental activation of adjacent elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 675: Add `padding: 8px;` or increase `width: 44px; height: 44px;` to meet the recommended 44px touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the focus indicator without providing a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see which account link has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 698: Add `outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);` or `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring);` to restore focus visibility.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on feature card links
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the focus indicator without providing a visible alternative. These "Learn more" links extend their click area via pseudo-element and require visible focus indication.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1006: Add `outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);` or `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring);` to provide focus visibility.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on FAQ accordion buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the focus indicator without providing a visible alternative. These expandable accordion buttons require visible focus indication for keyboard navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1085: Add `outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);` or `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring);` to restore focus visibility.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the focus indicator without providing a visible alternative. Custom-styled checkboxes require a visible focus state to indicate selection status to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1174: Add `outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);` or `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring);` to provide focus visibility.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer brand links
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the focus indicator without providing a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see when footer brand links have focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1200: Add `outline: 3px solid var(--colour-neutral-0);` or `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-neutral-0);` to provide contrast against the dark footer background.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on form checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] selector sets width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Checkboxes are frequently used form controls that must be easily activatable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1172: Change `width: 20px;` and `height: 20px;` to `width: 24px;` and `height: 24px;` or greater, or increase the min-height to 24px.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on social media links
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the focus indicator without providing a visible alternative. Social links in the footer require visible focus indication.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1224: Add `outline: 3px solid var(--colour-neutral-0);` or `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-neutral-0);` to provide contrast against the dark background.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on social media links
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a selector sets color: var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) on background-color: var(--colour-neutral-900) (#555555), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.8:1. This is below the 4.5:1 minimum required for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1220: Change the color to `color: var(--colour-neutral-300);` (#cbd5e1) or lighter to achieve at least 4.5:1 contrast.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the focus indicator without providing a visible alternative. Footer navigation links require visible focus indication.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1212: Add `outline: 3px solid var(--colour-neutral-0);` or `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-neutral-0);` to provide contrast against the dark background.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon-only buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon selector sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Icon-only buttons are frequently used and must meet minimum size requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 640: Change `min-width: 20px;` and `min-height: 20px;` to `min-width: 24px;` and `min-height: 24px;` or greater.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a selector sets min-height: 24px with no padding, placing it at the absolute minimum threshold without buffer. This provides no margin for error and may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate reliably.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1208: Add `padding: 8px 0;` or increase `min-height: 44px;` to meet the recommended 44px touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on navigation link hover state
issuesFound.explanation: The #nav-menu > li > a:hover selector sets color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) on background-color: var(--colour-info-light) (#dbeafe), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.8:1. This falls below the 4.5:1 minimum required for normal text, making the text difficult to read for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 575: Change the color to a darker shade such as `color: var(--colour-brand-primary-active);` (#003888) or darken the background to achieve at least 4.5:1 contrast.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on submenu link hover state
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a:hover selector sets color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) on background-color: var(--colour-neutral-100) (#f1f5f9), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1. This is below the 4.5:1 minimum required for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 631: Change the color to `color: var(--colour-brand-primary-active);` (#003888) or use `color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover);` (#004aaa) to achieve sufficient contrast.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on account navigation link hover
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:hover selector sets color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) on the header background (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.8:1. This is below the 4.5:1 minimum required for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 695: Change the color to `color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover);` (#004aaa) or `color: var(--colour-brand-primary-active);` (#003888) to achieve at least 4.5:1 contrast.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on FAQ content links
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dd p a selector sets color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) on background-color: var(--colour-neutral-50) (#f8fafc), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.7:1. This is below the 4.5:1 minimum required for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1093: Change the color to `color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover);` (#004aaa) or `color: var(--colour-brand-primary-active);` (#003888) to achieve sufficient contrast.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a selector sets color: var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) on background-color: var(--colour-neutral-900) (#555555), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.8:1. This is below the 4.5:1 minimum required for normal text, making links difficult to perceive against the dark footer background.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1208: Change the color to `color: var(--colour-neutral-300);` (#cbd5e1) or lighter to achieve at least 4.5:1 contrast against #555555.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on global anchor elements
issuesFound.explanation: The a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing an alternative visual differentiator such as border-bottom, font-weight change, or background highlight in the base rule or paired hover state. This makes links indistinguishable from surrounding text by colour alone, failing WCAG 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 133: Add `text-decoration: underline;` or `border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor;` to ensure links are distinguishable without relying solely on colour.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on account navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a selector inherits text-decoration: none and the hover state at line 695 only changes colour without adding an underline, border, or background highlight. Links are only distinguishable by colour, which fails WCAG 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 695: Add `text-decoration: underline;` or `border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor;` to the hover state, or add a background highlight to provide a non-colour visual differentiator.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on footer navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a selector inherits text-decoration: none and the hover state at line 1211 only changes colour without adding an underline, border, or background highlight. Links rely solely on colour change for differentiation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1211: Add `text-decoration: underline;` to the hover state to provide a non-colour visual differentiator.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on social media links
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a selector inherits text-decoration: none and the hover state at line 1223 only changes colour without adding an underline, border, or background highlight. These icon+text links rely solely on colour for differentiation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1223: Add `text-decoration: underline;` or `border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor;` to provide a non-colour visual differentiator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. An outline-offset applied to a zero-width outline renders nothing visible. Keyboard users receive no focus indication.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors media query
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block sets outline: none on :focus-visible with only outline-offset. In Windows High Contrast Mode, this removes the focus ring entirely, blocking access for high-contrast users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset. No visible focus ring appears for keyboard users navigating to the site logo.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes outline with only outline-offset. Keyboard users cannot see when this critical navigation control receives focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links remove outline with only outline-offset: -3px. Navigation links in expanded menus have no visible focus indication for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search input :focus-visible rule removes outline with outline-offset: 0. While :focus has box-shadow, :focus-visible specifically has no visible replacement, breaking keyboard focus indication.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes outline with only outline-offset. Icon buttons in forms must have visible focus indicators for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove outline with only outline-offset and border-radius. No visible focus ring appears for keyboard users accessing account features.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card "Learn more" links remove outline with only outline-offset. Interactive card links have no visible focus indication for keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Filter toggle buttons remove outline with only outline-offset. These aria-pressed buttons control product filtering and must show focus for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Billing toggle buttons remove outline with only outline-offset. These aria-pressed buttons switch between monthly/annual pricing and need visible focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table wrapper removes outline with only outline-offset. This region is tabindex="0" for keyboard scrolling and must show focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove outline with only outline-offset: -3px. These aria-expanded buttons must show focus for keyboard users navigating questions.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs remove outline with only outline-offset: 0 in the :focus-visible rule. While :focus has box-shadow, :focus-visible specifically has no visible replacement.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs remove outline with only outline-offset. Form controls must maintain visible focus indicators for keyboard form completion.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .footer-brand a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links remove outline with only outline-offset. Footer navigation links must show focus for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove outline with only outline-offset. These links provide important site navigation and must show keyboard focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links remove outline with only outline-offset. External links in the footer must show focus for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn min-height 32px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the 44px minimum recommended by WCAG 2.2 SC 2.5.8 Target Size. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 585: .btn { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px absolute minimum. Small buttons are difficult for users with limited dexterity to activate accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 643: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-width 20px, min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, both below the 24px minimum. Icon-only buttons require adequate touch targets for accurate activation.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 655: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; }
  Line 656: .btn-icon { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .nav-toggle min-height 28px, min-width 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle button sets min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px, both below 44px. The mobile menu toggle is a critical navigation control requiring adequate touch target size.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 691: .nav-toggle { min-height: 44px; }
  Line 692: .nav-toggle { min-width: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation menu links and buttons set min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommendation. Primary navigation items need adequate touch targets for mobile and touch users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 715: #nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: [id$="-submenu"] li a min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links set min-height: 28px, below 44px. Submenu items are often harder to target and require adequate touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 737: [id$="-submenu"] li a { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #search-input height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search input sets height: 28px, below 44px. Form inputs require adequate touch target size for users to activate and enter text accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 763: #search-input { height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"] width 28px, height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button sets width: 28px and height: 28px, both below 24px minimum. Icon submit buttons need adequate touch targets for accurate activation.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 785: #site-search button[type="submit"] { width: 44px; }
  Line 786: #site-search button[type="submit"] { height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .filter-tabs button min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Filter toggle buttons set min-height: 28px, below 44px. These interactive buttons control product filtering and need adequate touch targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1151: .filter-tabs button { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .pricing-toggle button min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons set min-height: 28px, below 44px. Billing cycle toggle buttons need adequate touch targets for accurate selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1259: .pricing-toggle button { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .product-body .btn-secondary min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Product card CTA buttons set min-height: 28px, below 44px. Product action buttons need adequate touch targets for user interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1309: .product-body .btn-secondary { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] width 20px, height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs set width: 20px and height: 20px, both below 24px minimum. Custom checkboxes need adequate touch targets for accurate selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1591: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a min-height 24px
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set min-height: 24px, at the absolute minimum but below 44px recommended. Footer links should meet the 44px target size for better accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1627: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .social-links a min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links set min-height: 28px, below 44px. Social links need adequate touch targets for accurate activation on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1637: .social-links a { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, clip: auto, and overflow: visible. This does NOT hide content visually while keeping it accessible. Screen-reader-only content will be visible on screen, breaking the intended design and potentially confusing users.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses space between words. This impairs readability for users with cognitive disabilities, dyslexia, or low vision who benefit from increased word spacing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 234: body { word-spacing: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn transform transition
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition properties. The :active state uses transform: translateY(1px). No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule disables this motion for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img transform
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img element uses transform: perspective(1200px) rotateY(-3deg) rotateX(1deg) with a transition. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 967-972 is empty and does not disable this motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card transform
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card uses transform: translateY(-2px) on hover with a transition. While line 1061-1064 has a prefers-reduced-motion rule, it only addresses the hover state transform, not the base transition property.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card a::after transform
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a::after pseudo-element uses transform: translateX(3px) on hover. Line 1095-1098 has a prefers-reduced-motion rule but only for the hover state, not the base transition.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .product-card transform
issuesFound.explanation: The .product-card uses transform: translateY(-3px) on hover with a transition. Line 1193-1196 has a prefers-reduced-motion rule but it only addresses hover, not the base transition property.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .stat-item transform
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-item uses transform: translateY(-2px) on hover with a transition. Line 1241-1244 has a prefers-reduced-motion rule but only addresses hover, not the base transition.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .pricing-card transform
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card uses transform: translateY(-2px) on hover with a transition. Line 1335-1338 has a prefers-reduced-motion rule but only addresses hover, not the base transition.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .faq-item dt button::after transform
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ button chevron uses transform: rotate(90deg) with a transition. Line 1543-1545 has a prefers-reduced-motion rule but it's incomplete and doesn't fully disable the animation.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. Many links in this stylesheet do not have hover underlines or other non-colour differentiators, meaning links are distinguished from surrounding text by colour alone, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Colour.
issuesFound.fix: Line 258: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Forced-colors mode focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the focus indicator is removed with `outline: none` and no visible alternative is provided, making focus invisible in high-contrast modes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 353: `@media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }`

issuesFound.title: Global focus-visible indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `:focus-visible` sets `outline: none` with only `outline-offset: 3px`, which does not create a visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 342: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`, which reduces the space between words and can impair readability for users with cognitive or visual disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 233: `body { ... word-spacing: normal; ... }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on focus-ring-inset utility
issuesFound.explanation: The utility class sets only `outline-offset: -3px` without an outline width or style, resulting in no visible focus indicator for elements using this class.
issuesFound.fix: Line 361: `.focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility class broken
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` utility class uses `position: static` instead of `position: absolute`, and lacks `clip` or `clip-path`, causing visually hidden content to remain visible in the layout and potentially overlap other content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 372: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Button transform animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The button class includes `transform` in its transition property, which can cause motion sensitivity issues, and is not overridden in the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block.
issuesFound.fix: Line 697: Remove `transform var(--transition-fast),` from the transition property, or add `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class sets `min-height: 32px`, which is below the 44px recommended target size and may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 696: `.btn { ... min-height: 44px; ... }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on focus-ring-brand utility
issuesFound.explanation: The utility class sets only `outline-color` without an outline width, resulting in no visible focus indicator because the global `outline: none` declaration takes precedence.
issuesFound.fix: Line 365: `.focus-ring-brand:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: Submenu link touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links set `min-height: 28px`, which is below the 44px recommended target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 867: `[id$="-submenu"] li a { ... min-height: 44px; ... }`

issuesFound.title: Icon button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The icon button sets `min-width: 20px` and `min-height: 20px`, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8.
issuesFound.fix: Line 777: `.btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; ... }`

issuesFound.title: Account navigation link focus indicator removed

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link has transform: translateY(-200%) and transition: transform that create motion. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 618 is empty and does not disable this motion for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.fix: Line 618: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid fails to set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility links the error message via aria-describedby but omits setting aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot identify the field as invalid, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1180: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the function body before or after the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function toggles the mobile navigation drawer but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, leaving users unaware the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2395: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus changes visible content but does not update an aria-live region. Screen reader users receive no feedback that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2618: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` in _openInstance. Line 2635: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` in _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens, the function updates the panel visibility but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This breaks the ARIA disclosure pattern and prevents assistive technologies from announcing the state change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2613: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion open() function expands the answer panel and updates CSS classes, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents screen readers from announcing that the panel is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4787: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving to the drawer or the first focusable element inside it. Keyboard users must manually tab into the menu, creating a barrier.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2407: Add `_drawer.focus();` or `getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]?.focus();` after opening the drawer.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close missing focus return
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it. Keyboard users lose their place in the tab order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2433: Add `_trigger.focus();` before the emit call at the end of the close function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function adds the open class and removes the hidden attribute from the drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves screen reader users unaware that the menu is now expanded, as only the close function resets the state to false.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function updates the UI and aria-expanded state but does not announce "Navigation menu closed" to the live region. The open function announces the state change, creating an inconsistent experience where screen reader users are not informed when the menu closes.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance() function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 as the state change is not programmatically determinable by assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function expands the answer panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger button. While the close function correctly sets it to false, the open state is not communicated, breaking the accordion pattern for screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion close function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.close() function collapses the answer panel but does not announce the change to the live region. The open function announces "expanded", but the close function remains silent, leaving screen reader users unsure if the action succeeded.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid to true
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. This means screen readers will not announce the field as invalid, significantly degrading the form validation experience.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent _onAccept handler does not announce decision to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent._onAccept() function saves the preference and hides the banner but does not announce "Cookie preferences accepted" to the live region. Users relying on screen readers receive no confirmation that their action was processed.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent _onDecline handler does not announce decision to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent._onDecline() function saves the preference and hides the banner but does not announce "Cookie preferences declined" to the live region. This lacks feedback for screen reader users compared to the visual disappearance of the banner.

issuesFound.title: pricing _syncToggleState uses aria-checked on native button without role switch
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState() function sets aria-checked on the toggle element. If the element is a native <button> (which is not assigned role="switch" in init), aria-checked is invalid; toggle buttons should use aria-pressed. This confuses assistive technologies about the widget's state.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation detects an invalid field, the markInvalid() utility links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the input element. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid, and assistive technologies that rely on aria-invalid will not identify the error state. The companion markValid() function removes the attribute, but since it is never set, invalid fields are never marked as such.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1480: Insert `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` inside the markInvalid() function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not update aria-expanded to "true"
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens visually and locks scroll, but the function fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, leaving users unaware that the menu is open. The companion close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an asymmetric state machine.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2128: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the `_open = true;` assignment inside the open() function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not update aria-expanded to "true"
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu panel is opened, the instance flag is set and CSS classes are toggled, but the trigger element's aria-expanded attribute is never set to "true". This violates the ARIA disclosure pattern and prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded state. The _closeInstance() function correctly sets it to "false".
issuesFound.fix: Line 2284: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;` inside the _openInstance() function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not update aria-expanded to "true"
issuesFound.explanation: Opening an FAQ accordion item animates the panel and sets internal state, but the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is never set to "true". This prevents screen reader users from knowing that the answer is expanded. The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", so the attribute exists but is never updated on open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4164: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` inside the open() function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` function adds the open class to the drawer and body but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. The corresponding `close()` function correctly sets `aria-expanded="false"`, creating an asymmetry where screen readers are informed when the menu closes but not when it opens.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close functions do not sync aria-hidden on drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` drawer visibility is toggled via CSS classes (`is-open`), but the `aria-hidden` attribute is not updated on the drawer element itself. When closed, the drawer should be removed from the accessibility tree to prevent screen reader users from navigating into hidden content.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance()` function removes the `hidden` attribute from the panel and adds the open class, but it does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. The `_closeInstance()` function correctly sets `aria-expanded="false"`, leaving screen reader users unaware that the dropdown has opened.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` function for FAQ items removes the `hidden` attribute from the panel and animates it, but it fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the question trigger button. The `close()` function correctly sets `aria-expanded="false"`, causing an inconsistent state for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The `markInvalid()` utility function links the error message via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input field. Without this attribute, screen readers do not announce that the field is in an error state, even though the visual error message is present.

issuesFound.title: productFilter _syncTabAriaStates does not update aria-pressed on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncTabAriaStates()` function toggles the `is-active` CSS class on filter buttons based on their state but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute. Screen reader users relying on ARIA states will not know which filter is currently active.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() fails to set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function adds an error description via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers cannot identify the field as having an error, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1371: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` before or after the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a dropdown panel, the function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger element. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2287: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation closes, no announcement is made to screen readers. Users navigating with assistive technology are not informed that the menu has closed, unlike the open() function which announces "Navigation menu opened".
issuesFound.fix: Line 2158: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` before the closing brace of the function (inside the if block or at the end before return).

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function expands the FAQ answer panel and updates visual state, but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function sets it to "false", creating an asymmetry where the expanded state is never communicated to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4172: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown menu updates the visual state and aria-expanded, but fails to announce the closure to screen readers. This is inconsistent with the open state which emits a custom event but no live region announcement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2313: Add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu closed');` after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', ...);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The module declares a `_trap` variable for focus trapping but never initializes it or activates it when the menu opens. Keyboard focus is not trapped within the mobile navigation drawer, allowing users to tab behind the modal overlay.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2107: Initialize the trap: `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });` and Line 2127: Add `_trap.activate();` after `_lockScroll();`

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates() missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The function updates the visual active state of filter tab buttons but does not update the aria-pressed attribute to reflect which filter is currently active. The HTML contract specifies these buttons should have aria-pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3847: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The open function adds classes and announces state but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger element. The close function correctly sets it to "false", creating an unbalanced state where screen readers are not informed the menu is open.

issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute and adds classes but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. The paired _closeInstance function sets it to "false", leaving the state inconsistent for assistive technology.

issuesFound.fix: Line 2525: Add cases for `case KEYS.ENTER:` and `case KEYS.SPACE:` that call `toggle(item)` and `e.preventDefault()`, similar to the pattern in dropdownMenu._onTriggerKeydown
