issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on validation state changes
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields are validated, the function updates visual styling and aria-describedby to link error messages, but it never sets aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or clears it (sets to "false") on valid fields. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state of form controls to users.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is toggled open and closed via the open() and close() functions, which add and remove CSS classes to show and hide the drawer. However, neither function updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger) to reflect the state change. Screen readers will not announce whether the navigation menu is expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons do not update aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons toggle between active and inactive states to show which category filter is applied. The function toggles CSS classes but fails to set the aria-pressed attribute to indicate the pressed state of these toggle buttons. Screen readers will not announce which filter is currently active.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce results via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and product cards are shown or hidden, the function updates the count badge and empty state visually but does not announce the results to screen readers via the liveRegion utility. Users navigating with assistive technologies will not know that the product grid has updated or how many results are now visible.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce billing switch to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly, quarterly, or annual billing periods, the prices update visually with animation, but no announcement is made to screen readers via the liveRegion utility. Users will not be aware that prices have changed or what the new billing period is.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels via _openInstance and _closeInstance, which manage the hidden attribute and CSS classes. While the register() function initializes aria-expanded to false, the open and close functions never update this attribute to reflect the current state. Assistive technologies will not know when dropdowns are expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is obtained from the DOM during initialization, but the code does not initialize the aria-expanded attribute to "false" to indicate the closed state on page load. Additionally, aria-controls is not set to link the trigger to the drawer element.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input receives aria-controls pointing to the suggestion listbox, but the aria-expanded attribute is never initialized to "false" on page load. The attribute is only set dynamically in show() and hide(), leaving screen readers without initial state information.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus are opened or closed, the visual change is not announced to screen readers. While aria-expanded is updated (once fixed), the context change of "Navigation submenu opened" or similar should be announced for clarity, especially when the dropdown content is loaded dynamically.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion items are expanded and collapsed via these functions, which update aria-expanded correctly. However, the content changes are not announced via the liveRegion utility. While aria-expanded provides state information, announcing "Answer expanded" or "Answer collapsed" provides clearer feedback for dynamic content changes.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions toggle the panel visibility but do not update `aria-expanded` on the trigger button (`inst.trigger`). The initial state is set in `register`, but subsequent toggles leave the attribute stale.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1830: function _openInstance(inst) { ... setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); ... }
  Line 1850: function _closeInstance(inst, returnFocus) { ... setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); ... }

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open` function expands the answer panel but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button (`item.trigger`). The `close` function correctly sets it to "false", causing state desynchronization.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2670: function open(item, announceToSR) { ... setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter._syncTabAriaStates` function updates the visual active class on filter buttons (`[data-filter-value]`) but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute. Screen reader users cannot identify which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2380: function _syncTabAriaStates() { ... qsa('[data-filter-value]', _tabsEl).forEach(btn => { const isActive = ...; setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive)); ... }); }

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter._updateCountBadge` function updates the text content and `aria-label` of the count badge (`[data-product-count]`) when filters are applied. However, it does not announce the change via a live region, so screen reader users are unaware the result count has updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2350: function _updateCountBadge(count) { ... liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`); }

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing.setPeriod` function updates price text and savings messages across all plan cards when the billing period changes. It does not announce this significant content change to screen reader users via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2520: function setPeriod(period, animate) { ... liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`); ... }

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid fails to set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markInvalid` function associates an error message with a field via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field itself. Assistive technologies rely on `aria-invalid` to announce that a field is in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1330: function markInvalid(field, errorEl) { ... field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); addDescribedBy(field, errorEl); }

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid fails to clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markValid` function removes the error description from a field but does not set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the attribute. The field may remain in an invalid state programmatically even after the error is cleared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1350: function markValid(field, errorEl) { ... field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false'); ... }

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function marks a field as invalid by linking an error message via aria-describedby, but it fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the input element. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In markInvalid, after addDescribedBy(field, errorEl), add: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer open and close functions add and remove CSS classes to show and hide the drawer, but they do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the visibility state. Screen readers will not announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In mobileNav.open(), add: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); Line: In mobileNav.close(), add: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close missing focus return
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, the function deactivates a focus trap that was never activated, and does not explicitly return focus to the trigger button. Keyboard focus is lost to the document body, requiring users to navigate back to find the trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Line: At the end of close(), after _unlockScroll(), add: if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item, the function animates the panel height and removes the hidden attribute, but it does not set aria-expanded to 'true' on the trigger button. Although the close function sets it to 'false', the asymmetry means the expanded state is never announced when opening.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In faqAccordion.open(), after item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden'), add: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu open and close instance functions manage the panel visibility by removing or adding the hidden attribute and toggling CSS classes, but they fail to set aria-expanded on the trigger element to indicate the dropdown state. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In _openInstance, after inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden'), add: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); Line: In _closeInstance, after removeClass(inst.panel, CSS.open), add: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the function updates the visible product cards and the count badge, but it does not announce the result count or filter changes via an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not notified that the content has updated or how many results are now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line: After filterAnimation.run completes (inside the onDone callback), add: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When switching billing periods, the price values update visually with animation, but there is no announcement to screen readers indicating that prices have changed or what the new prices are. Users relying on assistive technologies may miss the update.
issuesFound.fix: Line: After updating all plan cards in setPeriod, add: liveRegion.announce(`Prices updated to ${period} billing`);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation initialization finds the trigger button and drawer elements but does not initialize aria-expanded to "false" on page load. Screen readers will not correctly announce the initial collapsed state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line: After _initialised = true and event listener attachment, add: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing the invalid state from a field, the function removes the error message reference from aria-describedby but does not reset aria-invalid to "false" or remove the attribute. The field remains marked as invalid to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In markValid, after the aria-describedby cleanup block, add: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search initialization sets up the combobox role and event listeners, but it does not initialize aria-expanded to "false" on the search input element. Screen readers will not correctly announce that the suggestion list is initially collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: After mounting the suggestion listbox and creating the status element, add: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, the function does not move keyboard focus into the drawer or onto the first focusable element within it. Keyboard users remain on the trigger button and must tab through the rest of the page before reaching the opened menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line: At the end of the open() function, add: const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` function adds the `is-open` class to the drawer but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the `_trigger` button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the navigation menu is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1335: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.close()` function removes the `is-open` class from the drawer but fails to set `aria-expanded="false"` on the `_trigger` button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the navigation menu is now collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1355: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init function does not initialise aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init()` function does not set the initial `aria-expanded` state on the `_trigger` button. Assistive technologies will not know the initial state of the menu (closed). Also uses undefined `CONFIG.navigation.mobileBreakpoint`.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1405: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');
  Line 1415: const mobileBreakpoint = CONFIG.navigation.mobileBreakpoint || '(max-width: 768px)';

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance()` function removes the `hidden` attribute from the panel but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the `inst.trigger`. The state change is not communicated to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1530: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance()` function sets the `hidden` attribute on the panel but does not set `aria-expanded="false"` on the `inst.trigger`. The state change is not communicated to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1550: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register function does not initialise aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `register()` function sets `aria-haspopup` and `aria-controls` on the trigger but does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"`. The trigger's initial state is unknown to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1640: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open()` function expands the panel but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the `item.trigger`. Note that `close()` correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2375: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markInvalid()` function links the error message via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2980: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils markValid function does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markValid()` function removes the error description but fails to remove `aria-invalid` or set it to "false". The field may remain stuck in an invalid state for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3000: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce changes to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `applyFilters()` function updates the product grid and count badge dynamically but does not announce the change (e.g., "5 products found") to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1975: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce price changes to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod()` function updates all plan prices dynamically but does not announce the billing period change (e.g., "Prices switched to annual billing") to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2205: liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);

issuesFound.title: createFocusTrap activate checks undefined SUPPORTS.dom
issuesFound.explanation: The `activate()` function checks `if (SUPPORTS.dom)`, but `dom` is not a property in the `SUPPORTS` object. This prevents event listeners from attaching, breaking the focus trap entirely for modals and drawers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 830: if (document) {

issuesFound.title: viewportReveal init checks undefined SUPPORTS.reducedMotion
issuesFound.explanation: The `init()` function checks `if (SUPPORTS.reducedMotion)`, but the property is named `prefersReducedMotion` in the `SUPPORTS` object. Reduced motion preferences will be ignored, potentially causing seizures or discomfort.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3670: if (SUPPORTS.prefersReducedMotion) {

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator init checks undefined SUPPORTS.reducedMotion
issuesFound.explanation: The `init()` and `_animate()` functions check `if (SUPPORTS.reducedMotion)`, but the property is named `prefersReducedMotion`. Animations will play for users who requested reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3735: if (SUPPORTS.prefersReducedMotion) {
  Line 3720: if (SUPPORTS.prefersReducedMotion) {

issuesFound.title: searchResultsHighlighter references undefined REGEX.escapeRegex
issuesFound.explanation: The `_highlightTokensInElement` function uses `REGEX.escapeRegex`, which does not exist in the `REGEX` object. This causes a crash when highlighting search results, breaking the feature.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1775: const regex = new RegExp(tokens.map(t => t.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&')).join('|'), 'gi');

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField bind sets role button without aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The `bind()` function sets `role="button"` on the drop zone. While it handles Enter/Space, it acts as a toggle for the file input dialog but does not manage `aria-pressed` if it were to indicate an active drop state, though primarily it triggers a click. However, `aria-dropeffect` is deprecated.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3395: setAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-label', zoneEl.getAttribute('data-upload-label') || 'Upload files – click or drag files here'); (Ensure label is descriptive, current code does this but aria-dropeffect should be removed as it is deprecated).
  Line 3392: setAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-dropeffect', 'none'); (Remove this line as aria-dropeffect is deprecated).

issuesFound.title: Theme toggle icon button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component used in ThemeToggle does not receive an aria-label prop. Since the button contains only an icon (emoji) with no visible text, it has no accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 695: <IconButton onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} aria-label={LABELS[theme]} className="theme-toggle">

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button in SiteHeader controls the open state of the mobile menu (via isOpen) but lacks the aria-expanded={isOpen} attribute. Users cannot detect the menu state via assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 735: <button id={toggleId} ref={menuToggleRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-label={isOpen ? 'Close navigation menu' : 'Open navigation menu'} className="mobile-nav-toggle" onClick={isOpen ? close : open}>

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation internal trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion trigger buttons inside MobileNav control sub-menu visibility (via isExpanded) but lack the aria-expanded={isExpanded} attribute. The state change is not communicated to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 635: <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ item trigger button controls the visibility of the answer panel (via isOpen) but lacks the aria-expanded={isOpen} attribute. Screen reader users are not informed of the expanded/collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1265: <button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>

issuesFound.title: Filter tab buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter buttons in FilterTabs track active state via activeFilter but do not use aria-pressed to convey the selected state. Screen reader users cannot identify which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 965: <button type="button" className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>

issuesFound.title: Billing toggle monthly button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly billing button tracks selection via the value prop but lacks aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'}. The selected state is not communicated to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1135: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>

issuesFound.title: Billing toggle annual button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The annual billing button tracks selection via the value prop but lacks aria-pressed={value === 'annual'}. The selected state is not communicated to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1143: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>
